name: Cabezón Valley Loop
name_original: Cabezón de la Sal - La Cocina – circular por el Alto la Cerra y arroyo San Ciprián
description: This moderate 20.1-mile mountain biking route near Cabezón de la Sal offers varied terrain, including the Alto la Cerra and arroyo San Cipri
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cabezón De La Sal offers a diverse landscape for riders. The region features coastal paths with views of the Cantabrian Sea, river valleys, and elevated plateaus. Terrain includes a mix of natural trails, forest tracks, and routes that pass through historical mining areas. Best mountain bike trails around Cabezón De La Sal The most popular mountain bike trail is Acantilados El Bolao y playa de Luaña – circular por las minas de Udías, a 19.1 miles (30.8 km) moderate trail that takes 2 hours 31 minutes to complete. This route explores coastal cliffs, a beach, and historical mining landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Cabezón Valley Loop, a moderate 20.1 miles (32.4 km) path. This trail navigates the varied terrain of the Cabezón valley, offering a balanced ride. Local mountain bikers also love the El Moral Plateau – Ucieda Parking with Fountain loop from Cabezón de la Sal, a 73.9 miles (119.0 km) trail leading through plateau landscapes, often completed in about 11 hours 37 minutes. Mountain biking around Cabezón De La Sal is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and elevated plateaus, with options for various ability levels from moderate to difficult. The routes in Cabezón De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 1,300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cabezón De La Sal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Cabezón De La Sal?
A: There are over 60 mountain bike trails around Cabezón De La Sal, catering to various skill levels. This includes 4 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 26 difficult routes.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Cabezón De La Sal?
A: Yes, Cabezón De La Sal offers 4 easy mountain bike trails. While specific family-friendly routes aren't detailed, easy trails are generally suitable for those with less experience. For a moderate option that is popular, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000019313/acantilados-el-bolao-y-playa-de-luana-circular-por-las-minas-de-udias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acantilados El Bolao y playa de Luaña – circular por las minas de Udías&lt;/a&gt;, which explores coastal areas and historical mining landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced mountain bikers seeking a challenge, there are 26 difficult routes in the area. Notable options include the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau – Ucieda Parking with Fountain loop from Cabezón de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 119 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20502564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Ciprián Climb – Ucieda Parking with Fountain loop from Virgen de la Peña&lt;/a&gt;, a 75 km route with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that form a loop or circular route?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Cabezón De La Sal are designed as circular routes. For example, the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000043794/cabezon-valley-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabezón Valley Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.4 km path that navigates the varied terrain of the valley. Another option is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31846932" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ucieda Parking with Fountain – El Moral Plateau loop from Cabezón de la Sal&lt;/a&gt;, spanning over 110 km.

Q: What kind of scenery or viewpoints can I expect along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Cabezón De La Sal offer diverse scenery, from coastal paths with views of the Cantabrian Sea to river valleys and elevated plateaus. You can expect to see natural landmarks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2589035" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Bolao Cliff&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3725031" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Calderón Cove&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1507066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rabia Estuary&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass through historical mining areas, adding a unique cultural element to the landscape.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cabezón De La Sal?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cabezón De La Sal are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 40 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscape, which includes coastal paths, river valleys, and elevated plateaus, offering a balanced and engaging ride for various ability levels.

Q: Are there any historical sites or attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history and culture. You can find historical sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt;. Religious buildings like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Church of Oreña&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt; are also within reach, offering interesting stops during your biking adventures.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cabezón De La Sal?
A: The Cantabrian coast, where Cabezón De La Sal is located, generally experiences a mild climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, making them ideal for mountain biking. Summer can be warm, especially inland, but coastal routes benefit from sea breezes. Winter riding is possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wet conditions.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the mountain bike trails?
A: While many trails in Spain are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific routes, especially those passing through natural parks or protected areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5862315" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;. Generally, dogs on a leash are welcome on public paths, but always ensure they are under control and that you clean up after them.

Q: How long do mountain bike trails typically take to complete?
A: The duration of mountain bike trails in Cabezón De La Sal varies significantly based on distance and difficulty. Easy and moderate routes, such as the 30.8 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1000019313/acantilados-el-bolao-y-playa-de-luana-circular-por-las-minas-de-udias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acantilados El Bolao y playa de Luaña&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 2.5 to 3 hours. Longer, more difficult routes, like the 119 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31862846" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Moral Plateau loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take over 11 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on public trails in the Cabezón De La Sal area. However, if you plan to ride through private land or specific protected natural areas, it's advisable to check for any local restrictions or requirements beforehand. Always respect signage and local regulations.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. It corresponds to an STS S2 rating, meaning you might encounter small obstacles like roots and stones, uneven surfaces, and potentially need to push your bike on some sections.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the 'Cabezón de la Sal - La Cocina – circular por el Alto la Cerra y arroyo San Ciprián' is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same general area, bringing you back to your starting point.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail has a starting point conveniently located next to a parking area in Cabezón de la Sal, making it easily accessible for riders arriving by car.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: You can expect varied terrain typical of the Cabezón de la Sal region, which often includes trails through forests and along rivers. Given the name includes 'Alto la Cerra' and 'arroyo San Ciprián', the route likely features a mix of uphill and downhill sections with natural surroundings.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The region is known for diverse landscapes. You'll likely experience trails through forests and along the San Ciprián stream, with elevation changes offering views of the 'Alto la Cerra'. The broader area can feature incredible peaks and varied vegetation.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The Cantabria region generally has a Mediterranean climate. Mountain biking is popular here, so spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions for riding. Summer can be warm, and winter might bring colder, wetter weather.

faqs.question: Are there any notable points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features a significant Crossing of the Bustriguado Stream and includes the San Ciprián Climb, which are key highlights of the ride.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You will ride along parts of the Transcantábrica, Camino las Torcas, Cambera Navas, Puente Los Moros, Camino de Cueto, Turulonte, and Camino de Vaguero.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, specific leash rules or restrictions for this particular mountain bike trail are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs under control, especially when encountering other riders or wildlife.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride this mountain bike trail in the Cabezón de la Sal area.

faqs.question: Is there water available along the trail?
faqs.answer: Information about water sources directly on the trail is not available. It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your ride, especially given its moderate difficulty and length.

faqs.question: What gear should I bring for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for uneven surfaces and obstacles, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, appropriate riding attire, gloves, repair kit, first-aid supplies, and plenty of water and snacks.
